channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Mark Fuhrman, ex-detective convicted of lying during OJ Simpson trial, has died
item.description: Fuhrman was one of the first two police detectives sent to investigate the 1994 killings of OJ Simpson's ex-wife Nicole Brown Simpson and her friend, Ronald Goldman, in Los Angeles.
item.content:encoded:
Fuhrman was one of the first two police detectives sent to investigate the 1994 killings of OJ Simpson's ex-wife Nicole Brown Simpson and her friend, Ronald Goldman, in Los Angeles.

(Image credit: REED SAXON)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Three adult victims, two suspects dead after shooting at Islamic Center of San Diego
item.description: Three victims are dead, as well as two suspected shooters after a shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego.
item.content:encoded: Three victims are dead, as well as two suspected shooters after a shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Kori Suzuki

item.title: Why you should care about 2 power companies merging. Hint: affordability
item.description: NextEra Energy plans to acquire Dominion Energy to create the largest electricity producer in the United States.
item.content:encoded:
NextEra Energy plans to acquire Dominion Energy to create the largest electricity producer in the United States.

(Image credit: Marco Bello)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Son of radicals, Zayd Ayers Dohrn details a childhood underground and on the run
item.description: Dohrn's parents, Bill Ayers and Bernardine Dohrn, helped found the the Weather Underground. "I knew that the FBI was chasing us," he says. His memoir is Dangerous, Dirty, Violent, and Young.
item.content:encoded:
Dohrn's parents, Bill Ayers and Bernardine Dohrn, helped found the the Weather Underground. "I knew that the FBI was chasing us," he says. His memoir is Dangerous, Dirty, Violent, and Young.

(Image credit: David Handschuh)
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: The Supreme Court avoids taking up a fight over Voting Rights Act enforcement for now
item.description: After recently weakening the Voting Rights Act, the Supreme Court avoided for now taking up a legal question that may severely limit enforcement of the law's remaining protections for minority voters.
item.content:encoded:
After recently weakening the Voting Rights Act, the Supreme Court avoided for now taking up a legal question that may severely limit enforcement of the law's remaining protections for minority voters.

(Image credit: Jemal Countess)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: Judge dismisses Trump's IRS lawsuit, paving the way for a settlement
item.description: The president sued the IRS and the Treasury Department in January, demanding $10 billion over the leak of his tax returns years ago.
item.content:encoded:
The president sued the IRS and the Treasury Department in January, demanding $10 billion over the leak of his tax returns years ago.

(Image credit: Tasos Katopodis)
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: Alabama march traces path of the civil rights movement while gearing up for new fight
item.description: A civil rights protest in Alabama this weekend was organized to kick off a summer of voter mobilization and civic action across the South.
item.content:encoded: A civil rights protest in Alabama this weekend was organized to kick off a summer of voter mobilization and civic action across the South.
item.dc:creator: Joseph King

item.title: Why the Supreme Court's voting rights ruling could play a big role at the local level
item.description: The Supreme Court's recent ruling threatens the power of racial-minority voters in Voting Rights Act cases about not just Congress, but also at least 17 state and local governments, NPR finds.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court's recent ruling threatens the power of racial-minority voters in Voting Rights Act cases about not just Congress, but also at least 17 state and local governments, NPR finds.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: It takes a village – or a Phoenix suburb – to wrangle a wayward tortoise
item.description: When a large tortoise named Rex got loose, a Phoenix-area neighborhood went into a tizzy. More than just a fun commotion, Rex's daring getaway shows the challenges of sulcata tortoise ownership.
item.content:encoded:
When a large tortoise named Rex got loose, a Phoenix-area neighborhood went into a tizzy. More than just a fun commotion, Rex's daring getaway shows the challenges of sulcata tortoise ownership.

(Image credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Alina Hartounian

item.title: In Georgia primary, Republicans dominate spending and Democrats drive record turnout
item.description: Tuesday's primary in Georgia features contentious Republican contests for governor and U.S. Senate while Democrats hope an enthusiasm advantage is enough to flip two state supreme court seats.
item.content:encoded:
Tuesday's primary in Georgia features contentious Republican contests for governor and U.S. Senate while Democrats hope an enthusiasm advantage is enough to flip two state supreme court seats.

(Image credit: Brynn Anderson)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler
